ArchitectureBackend ArchitectureFrontend ArchitectureCLI Architecture
Architecture

Frontend Architecture

React application architecture and design patterns

Frontend Architecture

The TraceRTM frontend is a modern React application built with TypeScript, TanStack Router, and TanStack Query.

Tech Stack

Core Framework

  • React 18 - UI framework with concurrent features
  • TypeScript - Type-safe development
  • Vite - Fast build tooling
  • Tailwind CSS - Utility-first styling
  • shadcn/ui - Accessible component library

Routing & Data

  • TanStack Router - Type-safe file-based routing
  • TanStack Query - Server state management
  • Zod - Runtime validation

Graph Visualization

  • React Flow - Interactive graph visualization
  • D3.js - Advanced visualizations

Project Structure

Architecture Patterns

Component Organization

Components follow a clear hierarchy:

  1. Pages (pages/) - Route-level components
  2. Views (views/) - Feature-level components
  3. Components (components/) - Reusable UI components
  4. Primitives (components/ui/) - Base shadcn/ui components

State Management

Server State (TanStack Query):

  • API data fetching
  • Caching and invalidation
  • Optimistic updates
  • Background refetching

Client State (React hooks):

  • UI state (modals, selections)
  • Form state
  • Local preferences

URL State (TanStack Router):

  • Current view/page
  • Filters and search params
  • Navigation state

Data Fetching Pattern

Form Handling

Forms use React Hook Form with Zod validation:

Performance Optimizations

Virtual Scrolling

Large lists use virtual scrolling for performance:

Code Splitting

Routes are lazy-loaded for faster initial load:

Memoization

Expensive computations are memoized:

Graph Visualization

The graph view uses React Flow with custom nodes:

Testing Strategy

Unit Tests (Vitest)

Integration Tests (Testing Library)

E2E Tests (Playwright)

Accessibility

All components follow WCAG 2.1 AA standards:

  • Semantic HTML
  • ARIA labels and roles
  • Keyboard navigation
  • Focus management
  • Screen reader support

Example:

Build Configuration

Vite Config

Deployment

The frontend is deployed as a static site:

  1. Build: bun run build
  2. Preview: bun run preview
  3. Deploy: Static files in dist/ folder

Best Practices

  1. TypeScript first - Leverage type safety
  2. Component composition - Build from small pieces
  3. Custom hooks - Extract reusable logic
  4. Error boundaries - Handle errors gracefully
  5. Loading states - Show feedback to users
  6. Optimistic updates - Immediate UI feedback
  7. Accessibility - Design for everyone
  8. Performance - Measure and optimize

Related Documentation

  • Component Library
  • Testing Guide
  • Performance Guide
